description_long: This house features a rustic, weathered wooden exterior with horizontal planks and a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles. The structure consists of a main rectangular section and a smaller attached wing, both with matching wood siding and shingle roofing. The roof framing is visible from above, revealing exposed rafters and an unfinished attic space. There are minimal windows, with only a few small rectangular openings and a single round vent visible, giving the house a simple, utilitarian appearance. The main entryway is a plain wooden door set into the larger section, with no porch or decorative trim. The overall design suggests a traditional, rural architectural style, possibly inspired by early American or Scandinavian wooden houses.
description: A rustic wooden house with a steep gable roof, weathered plank siding, and minimal windows, featuring a main section and a smaller attached wing.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the gray shingle roof and the L-shaped layout of the house, with both main and wing sections visible.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the steep gable roof, plain wooden siding, and a small round vent near the roof peak.
description_view_2: Top-down cutaway view revealing the exposed wooden roof framing and rafters, as well as the main entry door and minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Side view showing the intersection of the main house and wing, with two small vertical windows and the black shingle roof of the wing.
attribute_entryway_design: Plain wooden door with no porch or decorative elements
